package transport

import (
	"net/http"

	"github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
)

// Positive scenario for request_dto_contains_gorm_tags.
// Anti-pattern: Request DTO carries gorm persistence tags,
// coupling the API contract to the database schema.

type CreateOrderRequest struct {
	ID         uint   `json:"id" gorm:"column:id;primaryKey"`
	CustomerID uint   `json:"customer_id" gorm:"column:customer_id;index"`
	ProductID  uint   `json:"product_id" gorm:"column:product_id"`
	Quantity   int    `json:"quantity" gorm:"column:quantity"`
	Status     string `json:"status" gorm:"column:status;default:'pending'"`
	TotalPrice float64 `json:"total_price" gorm:"column:total_price"`
}

func CreateOrderHandler(c *gin.Context) {
	var req CreateOrderRequest
	if err := c.ShouldBindJSON(&req); err != nil {
		c.JSON(http.StatusBadRequest, gin.H{"error": err.Error()})
		return
	}

	// Anti-pattern: the request DTO has gorm tags, leaking persistence
	// concerns into the transport layer.
	db.Create(&req)
	c.JSON(http.StatusCreated, req)
}
